title: Lycopodioides neocaledonica
taxonomy: Lycopodioides neocaledonica is classified within the plant family Lycopodiaceae, commonly known as the club moss family. This family is part of the larger group Lycopodopsida, which also includes other species of club mosses. Lycopodopsida is a subclade of the larger group Lycophyta, which is a division of vascular plants that also includes the division Sphenophyta. Lycopodiaceae is one of the oldest plant families known to date, with fossils dating back to the Devonian period. This classification is based on morphological and molecular characteristics that distinguish Lycopodiaceae from other plant families.

intro: Lycopodioides neocaledonica is a type of club moss, a primitive vascular plant belonging to the division Lycopodiophyta. The plant is valued for its traditional use in herbal medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as eczema, wounds, and fungal infections.
